insight: For Tunisia, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 minutes per break, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when Tunisia is not pressing before a break, they are able to regain some momentum afterwards, with an average change of +0.100 minutes per break, whereas when they are pressing, they tend to lose more momentum, with an average change of -0.200 minutes per break, and have not been able to maintain their momentum after the break. This implies that Tunisia's strategy and performance can be influenced by their approach before a hydration break, and they may need to adjust their tactics to better capitalize on these pauses in the game.
